title: Selection of Ampicillin Resistant Bacteria
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7fe2p","text":"Ampicillin is a β-lactam antibiotic routinely used in bacterial selection procedures to select for bacteria (usually E. coli) that have been transformed with an ampicillin resistance plasmid (pUC19, others). Ampicillin resistance is usually due to production of beta-lactamase enzymes which cleave the beta - lactam ring rendering the antibiotic inactive.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":10}],"data":[]},{"key":"3rl2b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6adbt","text":"The information below will outline preparation, storage, and a general selection procedure for ampicillin resistant bacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4282E3BED98C4B4685DC880BFA1DF31F","url":"http://store.p212121.com/ampicillin-sodium-salt/"}}}}

steps: An ampicillin stock solution can be prepared at a concentration of 100 mg/mL and should be stored at -20⁰C. Add 1 g (1000 mg) of ampicillin to 10 mL of dH2O 00:03:00 Ampicillin Sodium Salt P212121 Catalog #TWA-A-301
Sterilize the solution using a 0.22 μm filter Store solution in different aliquots at -20⁰C 00:02:00 Syringe Filter P212121 Catalog #LI-PES-0/22-13-S

LB-ampicillin agar preparation: Dissolve the following in 500 mL dH2O: 5g tryptone 2.5 g yeast extract 5.0 g NaCl 7.5 g agar 25 mg ampicillin Or 20 g pre-mixed LB agar powder (or LB agar capsules) 25 mg ampicillin 00:15:00 LB Miller Agar Capsules P212121 Catalog #RP
Boil solution on stirring hot plate for 1 – 2 min. 00:02:00 Hot plate and stirrer P212121 Catalog #BM-H4000
Autoclave for 20 minutes and let cool to 50-60⁰C. 00:20:00
Pour approximately 10 mL of molten LB agar into each plate. 00:03:00 Petri Dish P212121 Catalog #LI-PD01100
Allow plates to solidify for approx. 20 min. 00:20:00

Selection of ampicillin resistant bacteria: Using a sterile loop, take a sample of suspected ampicillin resistant bacteria from a colony or broth suspension and streak for isolation (using preferred method) on LB-ampicillin plates. 00:02:00 Inoculating loop P212121 Catalog #EM-65
Incubate plates inverted overnight (24 hrs.) at 37⁰C. 24:00:00
Any resulting colonies should represent ampicillin resistant isolates.
creator: Sean Seaver
